title: fagraea cambagei
intro: Fagraea cambagei, also known as the native or wild frangipani, is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Fagraea cambagei include its potential to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its use requires further research and caution due to potential interactions and side effects.
